title: humboltia boliviana
intro: Humboldtia boliviana, a species of flowering plant, is a member of the family Apocynaceae and is native to Southeast Asia. It is also known as the "mad dog tree" due to its toxic properties, but it has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimalarial effects. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and have also been shown to have antioxidant and antibacterial properties. Humboldtia boliviana can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, but its use should be strictly regulated and under the guidance of a qualified medical professional due to its toxicity.
